# Goblin Core

> Goblin Core is alpha, source-only C++23 server software that speaks the Redis RESP protocol and implements a focused subset centered on sorted sets (ZSETs). It stores the same sorted-set data in roughly 37% of Redis's memory (about 49 bytes per member) and serves it faster. This file is written for an operator installing, configuring, and running Goblin Core in production; it deliberately omits code internals.

Goblin Core listens on a TCP port and speaks RESP, so existing Redis clients and `redis-cli` connect unchanged. It supports `PING` and the sorted-set commands `ZADD`, `ZCARD`, `ZSCORE`, `ZRANGE`, `ZREVRANGE`, `ZRANK`, `ZREVRANK`, `ZREM`, plus two admin commands, `GOBLIN.MEMORY` (per-key memory introspection) and `GOBLIN.OPTIMIZE` (in-place compaction).

It is NOT a full Redis replacement. There is no persistence, replication, clustering, pub/sub, Lua, transactions, ACLs/auth, modules, key eviction, or any non-ZSET key type. Treat it as an in-memory, ephemeral, single-node sorted-set service that you rebuild from a source of truth on restart.

## Install and run

- Build from source (needs CMake >= 3.25 and a C++23 compiler such as GCC 14+/Clang 18+): `cmake -S . -B build-release -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && cmake --build build-release`. Optionally `cmake --install build-release --prefix /usr/local`.
- Run: `goblin-core --bind 0.0.0.0 --port 6379`. Defaults are bind `127.0.0.1` (localhost only) and port `6379`.
- Connect with any Redis client: `redis-cli -p 6379 ZADD leaders 42 alice`.

## Configure and operate

- Memory: budget about 49 bytes per member after `GOBLIN.OPTIMIZE` (about 2.7x less RAM than Redis for the same data). A single member is capped at 64 KiB.
- Deployment pattern: bulk-load a set, then run `GOBLIN.OPTIMIZE <key>` once before serving reads to compact it and repack the member index.
- Security: there is no authentication or TLS. Bind to a trusted interface only and put access control in front of it.
- Durability: none. Data lives in RAM and is gone on restart; reload from your primary store.

## Reference

- [Full operator guide (llms-full.txt)](https://github.com/adamdeprince/goblin-core/blob/main/llms-full.txt): self-contained install, configuration, every command and flag, and a production checklist.
- [README](https://github.com/adamdeprince/goblin-core/blob/main/README.md): build, run, command surface, and flags.
- [Benchmarks](https://github.com/adamdeprince/goblin-core/blob/main/BENCHMARKS.md): memory and throughput versus Redis, with methodology.
- [Performance and architecture overview](https://github.com/adamdeprince/goblin-core/blob/main/PERFORMANCE_BRIEF.md): where the memory and speed wins come from.
